commit.message:
cfl: retire 62 legacy patches, add CFL-001 (icAnsiToUtf8 HBO fix)

Retire all 62 CFL library patches to patches-retired/ — the patching
system caused repeated multi-hour rework cycles due to context conflicts,
false success claims, and upstream sync overhead. Build.sh now applies
only new targeted patches from cfl/patches/.

CFL-001: Fix heap-buffer-overflow in icAnsiToUtf8 (CWE-125/CWE-170)
- Root cause: CIccTagColorantTable::Read() reads 32-byte name[] without
  null termination. ToXml() passes to icAnsiToUtf8() → strlen() overread.
- Fix 1: Force name[31]='\0' after Read8() in IccTagBasic.cpp
- Fix 2: Defense-in-depth strnlen(szSrc, 256) in icAnsiToUtf8/icUtf8ToAnsi
- PoC: hbo-icAnsiToUtf8-clrt-multitag-IccUtilXml_cpp-Line394.icc
- Verified: patched icc_toxml_fuzzer handles PoC cleanly (exit 0, 0 ASAN)
- iccanalyzer-lite H144 already detects this pattern

Build.sh changes:
- Replaced zero-patch-mode comment with minimal patch loop
- Distinguishes 'applied', 'already applied', and 'FAIL' states
- Cleaned last CFL-083 include remnant from icc_deep_dump_fuzzer.cpp

All 18 fuzzers rebuilt successfully against upstream 1ffa7a8 + CFL-001.
SSD binaries refreshed at /mnt/g/fuzz-ssd/bin/.

commit.message:
fix: set keyVaultReferenceIdentity on Functions app to user-assigned MI

The Functions app uses a user-assigned managed identity, but without
keyVaultReferenceIdentity being set, Azure defaults to SystemAssigned
which doesn't exist — causing all @Microsoft.KeyVault() references in
app settings to silently resolve to the literal reference string.

This is why DISCORD_CLIENT_SECRET fails after every infra deploy:
it passes the literal KV reference string to Discord's token endpoint
instead of the actual secret value.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix race in RST_STREAM_IncompleteRequest_AdditionalResetFrame test (#65715)

* Fix race in RST_STREAM_IncompleteRequest_AdditionalResetFrame test

Move tcs.TrySetResult() after StopConnectionAsync so the response
handler stays blocked until the connection has stopped. This prevents
response HEADERS from being written before the GOAWAY frame, which
caused the test to intermittently fail with 'Expected: GOAWAY,
Actual: HEADERS'.

This is the same fix pattern applied in #65454 for the sibling
AdditionalDataFrames and AdditionalTrailerFrames tests, and in #57450
for the AdditionalWindowUpdateFrame test.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix RST_STREAM additional reset test ordering

Wait for the request to be aborted before releasing the application delegate gate in RST_STREAM_IncompleteRequest_AdditionalResetFrame_IgnoreAdditionalReset. This preserves the test intent while avoiding both the HEADERS-vs-GOAWAY race and the shutdown timeout.\n\nCo-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Made-with: Cursor

* feat(#288,#289): incremental extraction, startup guards, nano defaults, schema fix

## Priority 1 — Watermark-based incremental processing
- Add `scan_cursors` table to FactsDB (scan_type, last_session_ts, last_run_at,
  sessions_processed) with `getScanCursor()` / `updateScanCursor()` methods
- `getSessionFilePathsSince`: add optional `sinceTimestamp` param for cursor-based filtering
- `gatherSessionFiles`: add `sinceTimestampMs` param for cursor-based watermark mode
- Make `extract-procedures`, `extract-directives`, `extract-reinforcement`,
  `distill`, and `self-correction-run` incremental by default (only sessions
  newer than last run)
- Add `--full` flag to all five commands to force full re-scan

## Priority 1.5 — Startup guards & concurrency locks (#288)
- `SCAN_IN_PROGRESS` in-memory map: skip if scan already running
- 23-hour guard: skip if last_run_at < 23h ago; log with hours-ago + hint
- `acquireScanSlot` / `clearScanLock` helpers used by all scan operations
- Guards applied to: extract-procedures, extract-directives,
  extract-reinforcement, distill, self-correction-run

## Priority 2 — Default all LLM models to nano/cheap tier (cost safety)
- Add `OPENAI_NANO_CRON_MODEL`, `GEMINI_NANO_MODEL`, `CLAUDE_NANO_MODEL` constants
- `getDefaultCronModelLegacy` + `getDefaultPreferredModelList`: return nano-tier
  models when tier="nano" and no explicit config (replaces OPENAI_DEFAULT fallback)
- `runExtractReinforcementForCli`: change default extractionTier "heavy" → "nano"
- `runSelfCorrectionRunForCli` spawn: change fallback "heavy" → "default"
- `runAnalyzeFeedbackPhrasesForCli`: change "heavy" → "default"
- `trajectory-analyze` LLM call: change "heavy" → "nano"

## Priority 3 — Fix config validation rejects valid fields (#289)
- Add missing fields to `selfCorrection` schema in `openclaw.plugin.json`:
  positiveRulesSection, reinforcementLLMAnalysis, reinforcementToProposals,
  agentsRuleToProposals (all were in TS type + parser but absent from JSON schema,
  causing `additionalProperties: false` to reject them)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: TS errors in init-databases + update provenance test for expert preset default

* Fix scan lock leaks and no-op ternary in model selection

- Fix scan lock leak on zero-incident early return in runSelfCorrectionRunForCli
- Fix scan lock leak on empty session file list in runDistillForCli
- Add try/finally blocks to runExtractDirectivesForCli and runExtractReinforcementForCli for proper lock cleanup on errors
- Fix no-op ternary in getDefaultCronModelLegacy to return GEMINI_NANO_MODEL for nano tier

* fix: address Copilot review findings (lock leaks, dead ternary, delivery bug)

- Dead ternary: getDefaultCronModelLegacy now returns GEMINI_NANO_MODEL
  (not defaultModel) when tier is "nano", matching getLLMModelPreference
- Lock leaks: runExtractDirectivesForCli, runExtractReinforcementForCli,
  runDistillForCli, and runSelfCorrectionRunForCli now release the scan
  lock in a finally block so exceptions can't strand the lock
- Delivery bug: resolveCronJob strips the top-level `channel` field so
  plugin maintenance jobs never trigger a WhatsApp delivery attempt;
  delivery.mode is always set to "none" for these jobs
- updateScanCursor: rename parameter lastSessionTs → lastRunAt and add
  a JSDoc clarifying it's the scan completion time (watermark semantics)
- CHANGELOG: replace wrong [2026.3.91] entry with accurate description of
  incremental extraction, startup guards, nano defaults, and schema fix
- OPERATIONS.md: update extract-reinforcement default tier from "heavy"
  to "nano" (changed in v2026.3.91 for cost optimisation)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix scan cursor session timestamp tracking and add --full flag to self-correction-run

Bug 1 (dd43e1f6): updateScanCursor now properly tracks the newest session
timestamp (file mtime) in last_session_ts column instead of storing the scan
run time for both columns. Added getMaxMtime helper to calculate max mtime
from processed session files.

Bug 2 (d361d056): Added --full option to self-correction-run CLI command to
bypass the 23-hour startup guard. Updated CLI registration, handler signature,
and type definitions in register.ts and manage.ts.

* Fix cursor update inconsistencies in scan operations

- Remove !opts.full guard from cursor updates in runSelfCorrectionRunForCli to match other scan types
- Add cursor update before early return in runDistillForCli when zero sessions found to engage 23-hour guard

Implement RFC 9110 Range Request support for file serving endpoint (#480)

* Initial plan

* Implement RFC 9110 Range Request support for file serving endpoint

- Extract API handler into src/handler.ts for testability
- Create src/range.ts with Range header parsing (parseRangeHeader) and
  byte range resolution (resolveByteRange) per RFC 9110 Section 14
- Add HTTP 206 Partial Content responses with Content-Range headers
- Add HTTP 416 Range Not Satisfiable for unsatisfiable ranges
- Add Accept-Ranges: bytes header to all file responses
- Support int-range (bytes=0-499), suffix-range (bytes=-500), and
  open-range (bytes=500-) byte range specs

commit.message:
fix: pipeline section now fully driven by MSX milestones

fetch_msx_data.mjs:
- Added buildUncommittedPipelineList(): generates UNCOMMITTED_PIPELINE in
  the new list format directly from MSX milestones, filtered to:
  * Commitment != Committed (Pipeline/Uncommitted/Upside/Best Case/Unknown)
  * Status != Completed/Cancelled
  * Date falls in current month + next 2 months
  * Sorted by month then amount descending (TBD at bottom)
  * Status auto-mapped: Best Case -> on track, Uncommitted/Pipeline -> at risk,
    overdue Not Started -> blocked
  * amount_m converted from monthly USD to \
- generateTerritoryFile() now emits UNCOMMITTED_PIPELINE (list format)
  instead of only MILESTONES_BY_MONTH; MILESTONES_BY_MONTH kept as Section 3b
  for full audit trail

generate_report.py:
- add_uncommitted_pipeline_section(): if UNCOMMITTED_PIPELINE is absent or
  empty, falls back to MILESTONES_BY_MONTH and converts it on the fly,
  filtering to uncommitted milestones only — ensures all MSX data shows up
  regardless of which file format the territory uses
